home.page-title:
Know the conditions,
before you click 'Accept'.
home.center-text: Collections are organized groups of companies, arranged by industry, country, or language, making it easy to choose a specific niche of companies and explore their digital terms.
home.card-description3: Services used by Open Terms Archive
home.card-description4: Largest digital services used in France
home.card-description5: Major social media likely to impact the 2022 French elections
home.card-description7: Open to all contributions on generative AI services
home.card-description8: Largest digital services used in Germany
home.card-description9: Digital health services used in France
home.card-description10: Largest digital services used in Kenya
home.card-description11: Online intermediation services for businesses in Europe
home.card-extended1:
The Contrib collection gathers terms spanning a wide variety of industries and jurisdictions.

It was initially created thanks to a collaboration with TOSBack, which enabled the import of its data history.

It's the largest collections in the federation, tracking over 300 services maintained by volunteer contributors. It is also a good entry point into the Open Terms Archive ecosystem to become familiar with monitoring and change analysis.

The data in this collection has been reused in a variety of projects, from research into the impact of social media usage conditions on human rights, to improving the readability of documents.
home.card-extended2:
The Dating collection tracks terms of dating services used in Europe and Switzerland.

This collection is maintained by Marie-Pierre Vidonne (with the support of HestiaLabs until end of 2022) for the Dating Privacy collective.

It aims to raise awareness about privacy risks and build new practices in online dating for both users and platforms.
home.card-extended3:
The Demo collection tracks changes to the terms of use of services used by Open Terms Archive.

This provides a reference collection for best practices and enables the Open Terms Archive Core Team to be a user of the software it produces.
home.card-extended4:
The France collection tracks documents from the leading online services for French consumers.

The terms in this collection were initially declared by UFC-Que Choisir with support from the Office of the French Ambassador for Digital Affairs. They are now maintained by volunteer contributors.

The France collection has been used to detect irregularities in French online marketplaces, and demonstrated its value as a feasibility example for the TLDR Act.
home.card-extended5:
The France Elections collection was created for the 2022 French presidential election.

Its aim was to track the Community Guidelines of the five most widely used social media platforms in France that were likely to have a systemic impact on the conduct and outcome of the elections.

The collection was maintained and used by Reset Tech to monitor interference in France's 2022 presidential and parliamentary elections and to produce the joint research report "The French information ecosystem put to the test" with the Online Election Integrity Watch Group.
home.card-extended6: The France public services collection tracks changes to the terms of use of French digital public services.
home.card-extended7:
The Generative AI Contrib collection tracks terms of generative AI services and is open to all contributions.

It was initially created by the Sciences Po Law School Clinic's for the project "Artifical Intelligence (AI) Ethics and Regulation Watch" of the DIGILAW research program. Its aim was to assess the evolution of compliance of generative AI services with draft regulatory laws, and to study the impact of changes in the regulatory landscape.

At the end of the research project, the collection was taken over by volunteers and open to all contributions.
home.card-extended8:
The Germany collection monitors Terms of Service documents from major online platforms relevant to users in Germany.

This collection was developed by Hochschule Rhein-Waal and is maintained through ongoing academic work and volunteer contributions. It supports research, education, and transparency in digital consumer rights.

The Germany collection serves as a practical example of how structured repositories can support the analysis of digital service agreements within a national and legal context.
home.card-extended9: The "Numérique en santé France" collection is dedicated to monitoring the conditions of use of digital health services used in France, particularly when they are listed in the Mon espace santé service catalog.
home.card-extended10:
The Kenya collection tracks changes to the terms of use of the largest digital services used in Kenya and written in English.

This collection is maintained by Clifford Ouma and volunteer contributors.
home.card-extended11:
The Platform-to-Business Compliance (P2B) collection was originally created by PPMI and OpenEvidence to assess the application of the P2B 2019/1150 on behalf of the European Commission.

Following the success of the implementation, the European Commission adopted Open Terms Archive directly. It operates its own on-premises instance since December 2023 and publishes the Digital Services Terms and Conditions Database.
home.card-extended12:
The Platform Governance Archive (PGA) collection tracks the terms of major global social media services.

This data is maintained and analysed by the Platform Governance Archive at the Universität Bremen's Center for Media Communication and Information Research (ZeMKI).

This initiative offers researchers, journalists and citizens the tools to analyze how platforms structure and regulate communication and interaction in our societies.

It also aims to promote greater transparency and accountability of these powerful digital services.
home.sub-title: Open Terms Archive Manager lets users view and compare versions of digital service agreements, so they always know what they are agreeing upon.
home.document-tracking-description: Automatically track and archive versions of Terms of Service, Privacy Policies, and other legal documents from online services.
home.version-history-description: View complete version history with detailed diffs showing exactly what changed between document versions.
home.change-detection-description: Get notified when services update their terms. Never miss important changes that might affect your rights.
home.transparency-description: Promote transparency and accountability by making terms of service changes publicly accessible and verifiable.
home.about-section-description-1: Open Terms Archive is an open-source initiative that tracks changes in the terms of service and privacy policies of major  online platforms. By archiving these documents over time, we enable researchers, journalists, and users to understand how digital rights evolve.
home.about-section-description-2: This manager interface allows you to upload new documents, view version histories, compare changes between versions, and manage collections of tracked services.
home.upload-documents-description: Contributors select the terms of use to monitor, and our robots regularly download and publicly archive these documents.
home.view-versions-description: When changes are identified, they are recorded and made available for review by human analysts.
home.compare-changes-description: All versions are published as open datasets, enabling reuse, transparency, and research.
home.follow-section-description: Stay updated with the latest findings and highlights from Open Terms Archive and its ecosystem, including tracked changes and community contributions.
